title: Getting Started with meddra.read
text: MedDRA (Medical Dictionary Regulatory Activities) standardized medical terminology used clinical trials regulatory submissions classify adverse events. organized five-level hierarchy: MedDRA data proprietary requires license MedDRA MSSO. package helps load work licensed MedDRA data. examples use small, clearly fictional dataset bundled package illustration purposes.

title: Getting Started with meddra.read
text: download licensed MedDRA release, contains two subdirectories: files use $ character field separator.
code: my_meddra_release/ ├── MedAscii/          # Main MedDRA data files (.asc) │   ├── soc.asc        # System Organ Classes │   ├── hlgt.asc       # High Level Group Terms │   ├── hlt.asc        # High Level Terms │   ├── pt.asc         # Preferred Terms │   ├── llt.asc        # Lowest Level Terms │   ├── hlt_pt.asc     # HLT to PT linking table │   ├── hlgt_hlt.asc   # HLGT to HLT linking table │   ├── soc_hlgt.asc   # SOC to HLGT linking table │   ├── mdhier.asc     # Denormalized hierarchy │   ├── meddra_release.asc  # Version information │   └── ...            # Additional files (SMQ, specialties, etc.) └── SeqAscii/          # Sequential update files (.seq)     ├── soc.seq     ├── pt.seq     └── ...

title: Getting Started with meddra.read
text: Use read_meddra() pointing parent directory contains MedAscii SeqAscii (MedSeq) subdirectories. returns named list data.frames, one per file. result named list one data.frame per MedDRA file: data.frame corresponds one MedDRA source files. example, System Organ Class data: Preferred Terms: Lowest Level Terms (note: llt_currency = "Y" means term current; "N" means non-current synonym):
code: # For your licensed data, replace this path with your actual MedDRA directory: # example_dir <- "/path/to/your/meddra/release"  # The package includes a small fictional dataset for illustration: example_dir <- system.file("example_meddra", package = "meddra.read")  meddra_raw <- read_meddra(example_dir) names(meddra_raw) #>  [1] "hlgt_hlt.asc"               "hlgt.asc"                   #>  [3] "hlt_pt.asc"                 "hlt.asc"                    #>  [5] "intl_ord.asc"               "llt.asc"                    #>  [7] "mdhier.asc"                 "meddra_history_english.asc" #>  [9] "meddra_release.asc"         "pt.asc"                     #> [11] "smq_content.asc"            "smq_list.asc"               #> [13] "soc_hlgt.asc"               "soc.asc"                    #> [15] "spec_pt.asc"                "spec.asc"                   #> [17] "hlgt_hlt.seq"               "hlgt.seq"                   #> [19] "hlt_pt.seq"                 "hlt.seq"                    #> [21] "intl_ord.seq"               "llt.seq"                    #> [23] "mdhier.seq"                 "pt.seq"                     #> [25] "soc_hlgt.seq"               "soc.seq"                    #> [27] "spec_pt.seq"                "spec.seq" meddra_raw$soc.asc #>   soc_code                         soc_name soc_abbrev #> 1 10000100 Example Nervous System Disorders       ExNS #> 2 10000200        Example Cardiac Disorders       ExCD meddra_raw$pt.asc #>    pt_code              pt_name pt_soc_code #> 1 10001111     Example Headache    10000100 #> 2 10002111 Example Palpitations    10000200 meddra_raw$llt.asc #>   llt_code             llt_name  pt_code llt_currency #> 1 10001111     Example Headache 10001111            Y #> 2 10001112    Example Head Pain 10001111            N #> 3 10002111 Example Palpitations 10002111            Y

what: Joining into a Single Data Frame
title: Getting Started with meddra.read
text: join_meddra() merges hierarchy tables single flat data.frame, making easy look filter level hierarchy: resulting data.frame one row per LLT (Lowest Level Term) includes parent hierarchy levels. columns :
code: meddra_df <- join_meddra(meddra_raw) meddra_df #>   soc_code                         soc_name soc_abbrev hlgt_code #> 1 10000100 Example Nervous System Disorders       ExNS  10001100 #> 2 10000100 Example Nervous System Disorders       ExNS  10001100 #> 3 10000200        Example Cardiac Disorders       ExCD  10002100 #>                              hlgt_name hlt_code                     hlt_name #> 1       Example Neurological Disorders 10001110   Example Headache Disorders #> 2       Example Neurological Disorders 10001110   Example Headache Disorders #> 3 Example Cardiac Structural Disorders 10002110 Example Arrhythmia Disorders #>    pt_code              pt_name pt_soc_code llt_code             llt_name #> 1 10001111     Example Headache    10000100 10001111     Example Headache #> 2 10001111     Example Headache    10000100 10001112    Example Head Pain #> 3 10002111 Example Palpitations    10000200 10002111 Example Palpitations #>   llt_currency primary_soc_fg #> 1            Y              Y #> 2            N              Y #> 3            Y              Y

title: Getting Started with meddra.read
text: work terms specific SOC:
code: subset(meddra_df, soc_name == "Example Nervous System Disorders") #>   soc_code                         soc_name soc_abbrev hlgt_code #> 1 10000100 Example Nervous System Disorders       ExNS  10001100 #> 2 10000100 Example Nervous System Disorders       ExNS  10001100 #>                        hlgt_name hlt_code                   hlt_name  pt_code #> 1 Example Neurological Disorders 10001110 Example Headache Disorders 10001111 #> 2 Example Neurological Disorders 10001110 Example Headache Disorders 10001111 #>            pt_name pt_soc_code llt_code          llt_name llt_currency #> 1 Example Headache    10000100 10001111  Example Headache            Y #> 2 Example Headache    10000100 10001112 Example Head Pain            N #>   primary_soc_fg #> 1              Y #> 2              Y

what: Find all LLTs for a Preferred Term
title: Getting Started with meddra.read
text: find Lowest Level Terms (including non-current synonyms) given PT:
code: subset(meddra_df, pt_name == "Example Headache", select = c(llt_code, llt_name, llt_currency)) #>   llt_code          llt_name llt_currency #> 1 10001111  Example Headache            Y #> 2 10001112 Example Head Pain            N

title: Getting Started with meddra.read
text: Non-current LLTs (llt_currency = "N") historical synonyms. analyses want keep current terms:
code: current <- subset(meddra_df, llt_currency == "Y") current[, c("llt_name", "pt_name", "soc_abbrev")] #>               llt_name              pt_name soc_abbrev #> 1     Example Headache     Example Headache       ExNS #> 3 Example Palpitations Example Palpitations       ExCD

title: Getting Started with meddra.read
text: Standardized MedDRA Queries (SMQs) pre-defined sets terms used search adverse events. SMQ data available smq_list.asc smq_content.asc:
code: meddra_raw$smq_list.asc #>   smq_code             smq_name smq_level #> 1 10000001 Example Headache SMQ         2 #>                          smq_description     smq_source     smq_note #> 1 Example SMQ for headache-related terms Example source Example note #>   MedDRA_version status smq_algorithm #> 1              0      A             N meddra_raw$smq_content.asc #>   smq_code term_code term_level term_scope term_category term_weight #> 1 10000001  10001111          4          1             A           0 #>   term_status term_addition_version term_last_modified_version #> 1           A                     0                          0
